items.question: I _____ want to help you succeed in your career goals and aspirations fully.
items.translation: (Naprawdę chcę ci pomóc odnieść sukces w twoich celach karierowych i aspiracjach w pełni)
items.explanation: 'I do want' emphasizes sincerity of desire! Stronger commitment niż simple 'I want'. To mentorship language - pokazując genuine investment w someone's success. 'Do' makes support feel more reliable - not casual wish but committed intention.

items.question: He _____ study a lot, but he also makes time for hobbies and social life balance.
items.translation: (On faktycznie dużo się uczy, ale też znajduje czas na hobby i balans życia społecznego)
items.explanation: 'Does study a lot' acknowledges dedication while noting balance! Emphasizing że studying IS significant but not exclusive focus. To o well-rounded person - academic effort real but lifestyle balanced. 'Does' validates studiousness before highlighting other dimensions.

items.question: _____ remember to lock all doors and windows before leaving house every time!
items.translation: (Naprawdę pamiętaj żeby zamknąć wszystkie drzwi i okna przed opuszczeniem domu za każdym razem!)
items.explanation: 'Do remember' to urgent emphatic reminder! Imperative strengthened przez 'do' - shows importance. To safety warning - stressing criticality of habit. 'Do' makes instruction feel more serious - nie casual reminder but important security protocol requiring attention.

name: Struktury porównawcze - zaawansowane
description: As... as, not so... as, the same... as

items.question: This movie is just _____ good _____ the original one from years ago.
items.translation: (Ten film jest dokładnie tak samo dobry jak oryginał sprzed lat)
items.explanation: 'As good as' dla equal quality comparison! Oba 'as' - first shows degree, second introduces compared thing. Równa jakość - sequel matches original. To positive review - remake didn't disappoint. 'As... as' standard dla showing things są on same level - parity statement.

items.question: The new model isn't _____ expensive _____ the previous version was last year.
items.translation: (Nowy model nie jest tak drogi jak poprzednia wersja była w zeszłym roku)
items.explanation: Negatywne comparison z 'not as... as'! Showing nowy jest cheaper (less expensive). 'Not as expensive as' = tańszy. To price comparison - newer surprisingly more affordable. Negative 'as... as' dla showing inequality - one falls short of other.

items.question: She's _____ talented _____ her famous sister in music and performing arts.
items.translation: (Ona jest równie utalentowana jak jej słynna siostra w muzyce i sztukach wykonawczych)
items.explanation: 'As talented as' pokazuje równy talent! Obie siostry equally gifted - jedna nie overshadows drugiej. To o recognizing merit - younger/less famous doesn't mean less skilled. 'As... as' respects both achievements - refuses to diminish either.

items.question: His apartment is _____ the same size _____ mine, but better decorated nicely.
items.translation: (Jego apartament jest tej samej wielkości co mój, ale lepiej udekorowany ładnie)
items.explanation: 'The same size as' używa 'as' nie 'than'! 'The same' + noun + 'as' dla identical characteristics. Size equal ale decoration differs. To apartment comparison - one dimension matching (size), another differing (style). 'Same... as' dla equivalence statements.

items.question: Today isn't _____ cold _____ yesterday was - it's much warmer actually.
items.translation: (Dzisiaj nie jest tak zimno jak wczoraj było - jest znacznie cieplej faktycznie)
items.explanation: 'Not as cold as yesterday' pokazuje improvement! Temperature higher today. Negative comparison dla showing positive change - warming trend. To weather observation - contrasting consecutive days. Relief that conditions improved - 'not as cold' means more comfortable.

items.question: He drives _____ carefully _____ his experienced father does every day.
items.translation: (On jeździ tak ostrożnie jak jego doświadczony ojciec robi każdego dnia)
items.explanation: 'As carefully as' dla equal caution! Son matches father's safe driving - learned well. 'As... as' z przysłówkami dla manner comparisons. To compliment - young driver pokazuje maturity matching experienced driver. Skill parity despite age gap.

items.question: The test wasn't _____ difficult _____ I expected it to be honestly.
items.translation: (Test nie był tak trudny jak oczekiwałem że będzie szczerze)
items.explanation: 'Not as difficult as expected' = pleasant surprise! Anxiety was worse than reality. Negative comparison showing relief - feared worse. To o overthinking creating unnecessary stress. Reality less harsh niż imagination - 'not as bad as' common relief pattern.

items.question: My coffee is _____ the same temperature _____ yours now after cooling down.
items.translation: (Moja kawa jest tej samej temperatury co twoja teraz po ostygnięciu)
items.explanation: 'The same temperature as' dla identical state! Both coffees cooled do same warmth. 'The same' + noun + 'as' dla matching qualities. To casual observation - noting convergence. Initially different (jedna hotter) ale time equalized - now matching.

items.question: Learning English is _____ as important _____ learning any other useful skill.
items.translation: (Uczenie się angielskiego jest równie ważne jak uczenie się jakiejkolwiek innej użytecznej umiejętności)
items.explanation: 'Just as important as' emphasizes equality! 'Just' strengthens 'as... as' - dokładnie equally. To defending English learning value - not superior but certainly equal. Equality assertion - language learning deserves same priority jako other skills. Level playing field statement.

items.question: She doesn't sing _____ well _____ her sister, but she's improving quickly.
items.translation: (Ona nie śpiewa tak dobrze jak jej siostra, ale szybko się poprawia)
items.explanation: 'Doesn't sing as well as' acknowledges current gap! Sister better NOW ale improvement happening. Negative comparison with positive note - recognizing current inequality while noting progress. To encouraging - current deficit temporary, trajectory promising.

items.question: The second book was _____ interesting _____ the first one in the series.
items.translation: (Druga książka była równie interesująca jak pierwsza w serii)
items.explanation: 'As interesting as' dla maintained quality! Sequel didn't disappoint - matched first. To series review - consistency praise. Reader fear: sequels often weaker. Reality: quality sustained. 'As... as' celebrates maintaining standard across installments.

items.question: His explanation is _____ the same _____ what the teacher said in class.
items.translation: (Jego wyjaśnienie jest takie samo jak to co nauczyciel powiedział w klasie)
items.explanation: 'The same as what the teacher said' pokazuje alignment! His understanding matches official teaching. 'The same as' dla identical content. To validation - correct comprehension confirmed przez matching source. Shows successful learning - student absorbed lesson accurately.

items.question: Running a marathon is not _____ easy _____ it might look from outside!
items.translation: (Bieganie maratonu nie jest tak łatwe jak może wyglądać z zewnątrz!)
items.explanation: 'Not as easy as it looks' warns o hidden difficulty! Appearances deceiving - harder than seems. To cautioning przeciw underestimation. Marathon deceptively challenging - looking doesn't reveal full struggle. 'Not as... as' corrects false impressions.

items.question: Today's weather is _____ lovely _____ yesterday's was - perfect for outdoor activities!
items.translation: (Dzisiejsza pogoda jest równie urocza jak wczorajsza była - perfekcyjna na outdoor aktywności!)
items.explanation: 'As lovely as yesterday' celebruje consecutive good days! Two perfect days straight - luck continuing. To o grateful appreciation of sustained good conditions. 'As... as' shows consistency - quality maintained across time. Maximizing enjoyment while lasts.

items.question: This problem is _____ serious _____ we initially thought it would be originally.
items.translation: (Ten problem jest równie poważny jak początkowo myśleliśmy że będzie oryginalnie)
items.explanation: 'As serious as we thought' confirms initial assessment! Expectations matched reality - problem neither worse nor better. To validation of judgment - initial evaluation was accurate. 'As... as' shows forecasting był correct - no surprises, confirms original concern level.

name: Klauzule definiujące vs niedefiniujące
description: That vs which, przecinki czy nie

items.question: The book _____ is on the table belongs to my sister who studies literature.
items.translation: (Książka która jest na stole należy do mojej siostry która studiuje literaturę)
items.explanation: 'That' BEZ przecinków dla defining clauses! Identyfikujesz KTÓRĄ książkę - ta na stole (nie inna). Defining clause essential dla identification - bez tego nie wiadomo o której książce. 'That' preferred dla restrictive - narrows down which specific one meant.

items.question: My car, _____ I bought last year, is already having some mechanical problems.
items.translation: (Mój samochód, który kupiłem w zeszłym roku, ma już jakieś mechaniczne problemy)
items.explanation: 'Which' Z przecinkami dla non-defining extra info! Masz tylko jeden samochód więc 'bought last year' jest additional detail nie identification. Przecinki pokazują info is bonus. 'Which' in non-defining clauses. 'That' NIE może być z przecinkami - tylko 'which'.

items.question: Students _____ don't submit homework on time will lose points from grade.
items.translation: (Studenci którzy nie oddają pracy domowej na czas stracą punkty z oceny)
items.explanation: 'Who' BEZ przecinków dla defining! Określa KTÓRZY studenci - tylko ci którzy nie submit. Defining clause essential - rule applies selectively. Nie wszystkie studenci, tylko specific group (late submitters). No commas - restriction is point.

items.question: The Eiffel Tower, _____ is in Paris, attracts millions of tourists annually worldwide.
items.translation: (Wieża Eiffla, która jest w Paryżu, przyciąga miliony turystów rocznie na świecie)
items.explanation: 'Which' z przecinkami dla famous landmark! Eiffel Tower znany - lokalizacja (Paris) is extra info, nie needed dla identification. Everyone knows KTÓRA tower. Przecinki czynią Paris info supplementary. 'Which' dla non-essential additional facts.

items.question: The people _____ live next door are very friendly and always helpful to us.
items.translation: (Ludzie którzy mieszkają obok są bardzo przyjaźni i zawsze pomocni dla nas)
items.explanation: 'Who' BEZ przecinków dla defining neighbors! Specifying KTÓRZY ludzie - ci obok (nie wszyscy ludzie). Defining clause identifies specific group. Location (next door) is definitional - separates these people from others. No commas - location is essential identifier.

items.question: Her latest book, _____ she published last month, is already a bestseller nationally.
items.translation: (Jej najnowsza książka, którą opublikowała w zeszłym miesiącu, jest już bestsellerem krajowym)
items.explanation: 'Which' z przecinkami - 'latest book' already identifies! Publication date (last month) is additional context nie identification. Przecinki signal supplementary info. 'Which' w non-defining adds background bez being essential. Bestseller status is main news, timing is detail.

items.question: Chairs _____ are broken should be thrown away or repaired immediately for safety.
items.translation: (Krzesła które są zepsute powinny być wyrzucone lub naprawione natychmiast dla bezpieczeństwa)
items.explanation: 'That' BEZ przecinków dla selecting subset! Not all chairs - tylko broken ones. Defining clause critical - instruction applies selectively. 'That are broken' narrows which chairs meant. Safety rule targeting specific condition - restriction is key point.

items.question: Mount Everest, _____ is the highest mountain, is located in the Himalayas region.
items.translation: (Mount Everest, która jest najwyższą górą, znajduje się w regionie Himalajów)
items.explanation: 'Which' z przecinkami - Mt Everest znany! Height info (highest) is famous fact, nie needed dla identification. Everyone knows which mountain. Przecinki czynią superlative info explanatory. Location (Himalayas) is main wiadomość of sentence.

items.question: The restaurant _____ we went to last night had amazing food and great service.
items.translation: (Restauracja do której poszliśmy wczoraj wieczorem miała niesamowite jedzenie i świetną obsługę)
items.explanation: 'That' BEZ przecinków dla specifying! Którą restaurację - tę where went last night. Defining - identifies specific venue from many possible. Experience location essential identification. No commas - 'we went to' restricts which restaurant discussed.

items.question: My brother, _____ lives in London, is coming to visit us next weekend soon.
items.translation: (Mój brat, który mieszka w Londynie, przyjeżdża nas odwiedzić w przyszły weekend wkrótce)
items.explanation: 'Who' z przecinkami - assuming only one brother! Location (London) is extra info, nie needed identification (tylko jeden brat). Przecinki signal supplementary detail. Visit is news, location is context. 'Who' w non-defining dla additional information o known person.

items.question: Employees _____ arrive late repeatedly will face disciplinary action from management.
items.translation: (Pracownicy którzy przychodzą późno wielokrotnie spotkają się z działaniem dyscyplinarnym od zarządu)
items.explanation: 'Who' BEZ przecinków dla defining offenders! Not all employees - tylko chronic latecomers. Defining clause identifies who rule targets. Selective application - behavior determines who's affected. No commas - restriction to subset is entire point of policy.

items.question: The Internet, _____ has changed our lives, was invented in the late 20th century.
items.translation: (Internet, który zmienił nasze życia, został wynaleziony pod koniec 20 wieku)
items.explanation: 'Which' z przecinkami - Internet is unique known thing! Impact info (changed lives) is famous fact, nie needed identification. Everyone knows what Internet is. Przecinki czynią impact assessment commentary. Invention timing is factual news.

items.question: The keys _____ you're looking for are in my coat pocket hanging there.
items.translation: (Klucze których szukasz są w kieszeni mojego płaszcza wiszącego tam)
items.explanation: 'That' BEZ przecinków dla specifying lost items! Które klucze - te searching for. Defining identifies specific set among possible keys. Essential restriction - narrows to particular missing ones. Helpful location info requires first knowing WHICH keys - definition before solution.

items.question: Shakespeare, _____ wrote many famous plays, lived in England during Renaissance period.
items.translation: (Szekspir, który napisał wiele słynnych sztuk, żył w Anglii podczas okresu Renesansu)
items.explanation: 'Who' z przecinkami - Shakespeare is znany unique person! Writing plays is famous fact, nie needed identification. Everyone knows who Shakespeare is. Przecinki czynią accomplishment background. Residence info is historical detail being taught.

items.question: People _____ exercise regularly tend to live longer and healthier lives overall.
items.translation: (Ludzie którzy ćwiczą regularnie zwykle żyją dłużej i zdrowiej ogólnie)
items.explanation: 'Who' BEZ przecinków dla defining healthy group! Not all people - tylko ci who exercise. Defining clause identifies subset with specific behavior. Health correlation applies selectively. No commas - restriction to exercisers is crucial dla meaningful health statement.

name: Przeszłe nawyki - 'would' vs 'used to'
description: Różnice w opisywaniu przeszłych zwyczajów

items.question: When I was a child, I _____ live in a small village in the countryside.
items.translation: (Kiedy byłem dzieckiem, mieszkałem w małej wiosce na wsi)
items.explanation: 'Used to' dla past STATES (nie tylko actions)! 'Live' opisuje gdzie byłeś (state) nie repeated action. 'Would' NIE działa dla states - tylko dla habits. To o past residence - where you were, nie co repeatedly did. 'Used to' única opcja dla states of being.

items.question: My grandmother _____ tell us fascinating stories about her youth every evening.
items.translation: (Moja babcia opowiadała nam fascynujące historie o jej młodości każdego wieczoru)
items.explanation: 'Would' LUB 'used to' dla repeated past actions! Oba działają ale 'would' bardziej nostalgic i literary. Storytelling (repeated activity) każdego wieczoru. To fond memory - habitual evening ritual. 'Would' adds wistful tone - warm recollection of repeated cherished moments.

items.question: I _____ be very shy, but now I'm much more confident in social situations.
items.translation: (Byłem bardzo nieśmiały, ale teraz jestem znacznie bardziej pewny w sytuacjach społecznych)
items.explanation: 'Used to be shy' dla past personality STATE! 'Be shy' opisuje character nie action, więc tylko 'used to'. To o personality transformation - past trait changed. Contrast: used to be X, now Y. Shows personal growth - overcoming past limitation.

items.question: We _____ go swimming in the lake every hot summer afternoon together.
items.translation: (Chodziliśmy pływać w jeziorze każdego gorącego letniego popołudnia razem)
items.explanation: 'Would go swimming' dla nostalgic repeated action! 'Would' + activity creates wistful memory feel. Swimming (repeated fun) każdego afternoon. To childhood summer memory - regular joyful ritual. 'Would' more evocative niż 'used to' - paints vivid recurring scene.

items.question: There _____ be a cinema on this corner, but it closed down years ago.
items.translation: (Kiedyś było kino na tym rogu, ale zamknęło się lata temu)
items.explanation: 'There used to be' dla past existence! Było (state of existing) nie action. 'Would' NIE działa z 'there is/was'. To o changed neighborhood - building/business gone. 'Used to' unique dla existence statements - couldn't say 'would be' here.

items.question: He _____ play the guitar beautifully, but he gave it up after injury.
items.translation: (Grał pięknie na gitarze, ale porzucił to po kontuzji)
items.explanation: 'Used to play' podkreśla że stopped! 'Used to' implies nie longer doing - discontinued habit. 'Would play' wouldn't show cessation clearly. To o lost hobby - injury ended musical pursuit. 'Used to' perfect dla emphasizing contrast: did before, doesn't now.

items.question: On Sundays, we _____ have big family dinners with all relatives gathered.
items.translation: (W niedziele mieliśmy duże rodzinne obiady ze wszystkimi krewnymi zebranymi)
items.explanation: 'Would have dinners' dla nostalgic Sunday ritual! Repeated family tradition (action). 'Would' creates warm tone - beloved routine. Weekly gathering (regular event) portrayed fondly. 'Would' adds emotional warmth to memory - 'used to' more neutral factual.

items.question: I _____ believe in Santa Claus when I was a young innocent child.
items.translation: (Wierzyłem w Świętego Mikołaja kiedy byłem młodym niewinnym dzieckiem)
items.explanation: 'Used to believe' dla past mental STATE! Belief (state of mind) nie action. 'Would' NIE działa dla states. To childhood innocence memory - belief changed z wiekiem. Loss of belief natural development - 'used to' marks transition from believing to knowing.

items.question: My father _____ take us fishing by the river early every Saturday morning.
items.translation: (Mój ojciec zabierał nas na ryby nad rzekę wcześnie każdej soboty rano)
items.explanation: 'Would take us fishing' dla cherished repeated activity! Father-child bonding ritual. 'Would' creates nostalgia - treasured recurring memory. Weekly tradition (repeated experience) recalled warmly. 'Would' adds sentimental quality - painting loving family scene repeatedly experienced.

items.question: This building _____ be a hospital, but now it's luxury apartment complex.
items.translation: (Ten budynek kiedyś był szpitalem, ale teraz to luksusowy kompleks apartamentów)
items.explanation: 'Used to be hospital' dla past function! Building WAS hospital (state/identity) nie action. 'Would' NIE działa for states of being. To o repurposing - building changed use. Urban development story - medical facility became residential. 'Used to' marks identity transformation.

items.question: Whenever it rained, the children _____ jump in puddles and get completely muddy.
items.translation: (Kiedykolwiek padało, dzieci skakały w kałuże i brudziły się całkowicie)
items.explanation: 'Whenever' + 'would' dla conditional habits! Rain (condition) triggered puddle jumping (response). 'Would' + 'whenever/when' creates pattern - certain circumstance reliably produced behavior. Childhood playfulness memory - rain wasn't obstacle but invitation. Conditional habitual pattern.

items.question: I _____ to spicy food now after living in Mexico for three years.
items.translation: (Jestem przyzwyczajony do ostrego jedzenia teraz po mieszkaniu w Meksyku przez trzy lata)
items.explanation: 'Am used to' = accustomed NOW (NIE past habit)! 'Be used to' o current adaptation nie 'used to' (past). Developed tolerance przez exposure. To acquired taste - prolonged experience created comfort. Different structure entirely - 'am used to' present state, 'used to' past habit.

items.question: She _____ walk to school every day, but now she takes the bus instead.
items.translation: (Chodziła do szkoły każdego dnia, ale teraz jeździ autobusem zamiast)
items.explanation: 'Used to walk' emphasizes change! Past habit (walking) contrasted z present habit (bus). 'Used to' + 'but now' structure perfect dla showing transitions. To lifestyle change - transportation method evolved. Clear before/after - 'used to' highlights discontinued practice.

items.question: On summer evenings, we _____ sit outside and watch the beautiful sunset together.
items.translation: (Letnimi wieczorami siadaliśmy na zewnątrz i oglądaliśmy piękny zachód słońca razem)
items.explanation: 'Would sit and watch' dla repeated pleasant ritual! Summer evening routine (seasonal habit). 'Would' paints recurring peaceful scene - nostalgia for simple pleasures. Repeated enjoyment of nature. 'Would' adds warmth - treasured memory of regular shared moments.

items.question: There _____ be much more green space here, before all the construction started.
items.translation: (Kiedyś było znacznie więcej zielonej przestrzeni tutaj, zanim cała budowa się zaczęła)
items.explanation: 'Used to be more space' dla past environmental state! Existence of space (state) nie action. 'Would' NIE działa. To o urban development regret - green areas lost to construction. Environmental change documented - 'used to' marks co was before transformation eliminated it.

name: Podmiot formalny 'it' vs 'there'
description: It's raining vs There's a problem

items.question: _____ is raining heavily outside - don't forget to take your umbrella with you!
items.translation: (Pada mocno na zewnątrz - nie zapomnij wziąć parasola ze sobą!)
items.explanation: 'It' dla weather! Zawsze 'it rains/snows' nie 'there rains'. 'It' jako dummy subject dla weather phenomena - standardowa konstrukcja. To weather warning - heavy rain means umbrella needed. Fixed pattern: It + weather verb - universal English rule.

items.question: _____ are many reasons why people choose to learn new languages throughout life.
items.translation: (Jest wiele powodów dlaczego ludzie wybierają uczenie się nowych języków przez życie)
items.explanation: 'There are' wprowadza existence of things! 'There' + be + noun dla talking o co exists. Multiple reasons exist - 'there are' presents them. To introduction do explanation - announcing presence before listing. 'There' dla bringing new information into discussion.

items.question: _____ takes about two hours to drive from here to the nearest big city.
items.translation: (Zajmuje około dwóch godzin jazdy stąd do najbliższego dużego miasta)
items.explanation: 'It takes' dla time/effort required! 'It' jako dummy dla distance/duration statements. Not 'there takes' - 'it' standard dla expressing requirements. To practical travel info - setting expectations dla journey length. Fixed expression: It + takes + time.

items.question: _____ seems to be a problem with your internet connection right now.
items.translation: (Wydaje się być problem z twoim połączeniem internetowym właśnie teraz)
items.explanation: 'There seems to be' introduces discovered problem! 'There' + seems + to be dla noticing existence. Problem emerged - 'there' announces its presence. To tech troubleshooting observation - identifying issue existence before diagnosing cause. 'There' brings problem into awareness.

items.question: _____ is getting dark early these days - winter is approaching soon now.
items.translation: (Robi się ciemno wcześnie w te dni - zima się zbliża wkrótce teraz)
items.explanation: 'It's getting dark' dla light conditions! 'It' dla daylight/darkness statements. Seasonal change observation - days shortening. Not 'there's getting' - 'it' standard dla environmental conditions. Fixed pattern: It + gets + adjective (dark/light/cold/warm).

items.question: _____ was a lot of traffic on the highway this morning during rush hour.
items.translation: (Był duży ruch na autostradzie dziś rano podczas godzin szczytu)
items.explanation: 'There was traffic' introduces past situation! 'There' + be dla describing co existed. Congestion (existed condition) explained. To commute complaint - reporting encountered obstacle. 'There' presents situation as discovered fact - you encountered it.

items.question: _____ is important to arrive on time for job interviews always without exception.
items.translation: (Jest ważne żeby przybyć na czas na rozmowy kwalifikacyjne zawsze bez wyjątku)
items.explanation: 'It is important to' używa 'it' jako placeholder! Real subject 'to arrive on time' delayed. 'It' holds subject position. To professional advice - punctuality critical. Fixed structure: It + be + adjective + to infinitive. Formal way of stating necessities.

items.question: _____ isn't enough time to finish all this work before the deadline tomorrow.
items.translation: (Nie ma wystarczająco czasu żeby skończyć całą tę pracę przed terminem jutro)
items.explanation: 'There isn't enough time' o insufficiency! 'There' + be dla quantity/availability statements. Not enough exists - scarcity. To time pressure complaint - insufficient resources. 'There' announces lack - bringing inadequacy into focus requiring response.

items.question: _____ was nice meeting you today - I hope we can work together soon!
items.translation: (Miło było spotkać cię dzisiaj - mam nadzieję że możemy pracować razem wkrótce!)
items.explanation: 'It was nice meeting' używa 'it' dla experiences! 'It' jako dummy dla describing encounter. Polite closing phrase - expressing pleasure. Fixed expression: It + was + adjective + gerund. Professional networking courtesy - positive impression statement.

items.question: _____ is no excuse for being rude to others, regardless of circumstances always.
items.translation: (Nie ma wymówki dla bycia niegrzecznym dla innych, niezależnie od okoliczności zawsze)
items.explanation: 'There is no excuse' negates justification! 'There' + be + no dla absolute denials. Zero tolerance statement - no valid reason exists. To moral absolute - behavior inexcusable under any conditions. 'There is no' creates categorical rejection.

items.question: _____ sounds like someone is knocking on the front door loudly right now.
items.translation: (Brzmi jakby ktoś pukał do frontowych drzwi głośno właśnie teraz)
items.explanation: 'It sounds like' dla perceptions! 'It' dla describing co you hear/sense. Auditory observation - noticing sound. Not 'there sounds' - 'it' standard dla sensory reports. Alerting to possible visitor - sound interpretation shared.

items.question: _____ must be a mistake in these calculations - the numbers don't add up correctly.
items.translation: (Musi być błąd w tych obliczeniach - liczby się nie zgadzają poprawnie)
items.explanation: 'There must be' deduces existence! Logic suggests error exists. 'There' + modal dla confident assumptions o presence. Numbers wrong means mistake somewhere. To detective work - evidence (wrong sums) proves something (error) must exist.

items.question: _____ is freezing cold in this room - can we turn on the heating please?
items.translation: (Jest lodowato zimno w tym pokoju - czy możemy włączyć ogrzewanie proszę?)
items.explanation: 'It's freezing' dla temperature sensation! 'It' dla environmental conditions. Cold level unbearable - requesting heat. Not 'there is freezing' - 'it' standard dla temp/comfort statements. Physical discomfort expressed through 'it' + adjective pattern.

items.question: _____ seems to be some confusion about the meeting schedule and location details.
items.translation: (Wydaje się być jakieś zamieszanie o harmonogramie spotkania i szczegółach lokalizacji)
items.explanation: 'There seems to be' introduces apparent problem! 'There' + seems dla uncertain existence. Confusion apparent (noticed) - 'there' presents observed issue. To clarification needed situation - announcing presence of misunderstanding requiring resolution. Diplomatic problem identification.

items.question: _____ is difficult to learn a new language without consistent regular practice daily.
items.translation: (Jest trudno nauczyć się nowego języka bez konsekwentnej regularnej praktyki codziennie)
items.explanation: 'It is difficult to learn' używa 'it' placeholder! Real subject 'to learn' delayed. 'It' formal subject structure. To language learning truth - practice necessity. Fixed: It + be + adjective + infinitive. General statement o learning requirements.

name: Rzeczowniki abstrakcyjne vs konkretne z artykułami
description: Love vs the love, life vs a life

items.question: _____ love is the most powerful emotion in the world, stronger than anything.
items.translation: (Miłość jest najpotężniejszą emocją na świecie, silniejszą niż cokolwiek)
items.explanation: Zero article dla abstract concept 'love' generally! Mówiąc o miłości jako koncepcji (not specific instance). Universal truth o emocjach. Abstract nouns bez przedimka kiedy discussing concept itself. 'Love' (general idea) różne od 'the love' (specific instance). Philosophical general statement.

items.question: _____ love between a mother and child is incredibly special and unique bond.
items.translation: (Miłość między matką a dzieckiem jest niesamowicie specjalnym i unikalnym związkiem)
items.explanation: 'The love between' dla specific type! Defined BY relationship (mother-child) więc 'the'. Restricted context czyni concrete. Not love generally ale specific kind. 'The' kiedy abstract noun restricted by phrase - becomes defined specific thing.

items.question: He lived _____ interesting life full of adventures and amazing experiences worldwide.
items.translation: (Żył ciekawym życiem pełnym przygód i niesamowitych doświadczeń na świecie)
items.explanation: 'An interesting life' używa article z adjective! 'Life' generally bez przedimka ale z przymiotnikiem gets 'a/an'. Describes kind of life - interesting type. Abstract noun staje się countable kiedy qualified. 'An' categorizes life type - turning philosophical concept into described entity.

items.question: _____ happiness cannot be bought with money or material possessions at all.
items.translation: (Szczęścia nie można kupić za pieniądze lub materialne dobra wcale)
items.explanation: Zero dla 'happiness' as abstract concept! General truth o nature of joy. Universal principle - happiness transcends money. Abstract emotional states bez przedimka w general statements. Philosophical truth o co money can't buy - concept-level discussion.

items.question: She found _____ happiness in simple everyday things like morning coffee and walks.
items.translation: (Znalazła szczęście w prostych codziennych rzeczach jak poranna kawa i spacery)
items.explanation: Zero dla 'found happiness' - discovering emotion! 'Find' + abstract emotion zazwyczaj bez przedimka. Personal discovery of joy. 'Happiness' here as experiential state nie countable thing. Finding emotional fulfillment - abstract achievement expressed bez article.

items.question: _____ life is too short to waste on negativity and unimportant conflicts daily.
items.translation: (Życie jest zbyt krótkie żeby marnować na negatywność i nieważne konflikty codziennie)
items.explanation: Zero dla 'life' as existence concept! Philosophical statement o życiu generally. Universal wisdom. Abstract noun bez przedimka discussing fundamental nature. 'Life' (existence itself) różni się od 'a life' (someone's biography). Conceptual level - general truth.

items.question: He has _____ deep love for classical music and attends concerts regularly often.
items.translation: (Ma głęboką miłość do muzyki klasycznej i regularnie często uczęszcza na koncerty)
items.explanation: 'A deep love' używa article z adjective! 'Love' modified by 'deep' becomes countable instance. Describes type/intensity of emotion. Abstract + adjective staje się describable entity. 'A love' (specific passion) różni się od 'love' (concept). Qualification makes abstract concrete.

items.question: _____ death is a natural part of life that everyone must face eventually someday.
items.translation: (Śmierć jest naturalną częścią życia którą wszyscy muszą w końcu kiedyś stawić czoła)
items.explanation: Zero dla 'death' as universal concept! Philosophical truth o mortality. Abstract inevitable process bez przedimka. Discussing death generally (not specific death). Universal human condition - concept-level statement o life's nature.

items.question: She showed _____ great patience with the difficult students during the long lesson.
items.translation: (Pokazała wielką cierpliwość z trudnymi uczniami podczas długiej lekcji)
items.explanation: Zero dla 'showed patience' - displaying quality! 'Show/demonstrate' + abstract quality zazwyczaj bez przedimka. Exhibiting virtue. 'Patience' as demonstrated characteristic nie counted thing. Behavioral display - quality manifested expressed bez article.

items.question: _____ time heals all wounds, both physical and emotional ones eventually.
items.translation: (Czas leczy wszystkie rany, zarówno fizyczne jak i emocjonalne w końcu)
items.explanation: Zero dla 'time' as abstract force! Philosophical saying o healing power. Time as concept (not measured duration). Proverb-like wisdom - time's therapeutic nature. Abstract force bez przedimka w universal truths - discussing time's nature generally.

items.question: He lived _____ life of luxury and comfort without any financial worries ever.
items.translation: (Żył życiem luksusu i komfortu bez żadnych finansowych zmartwień kiedykolwiek)
items.explanation: 'A life of luxury' opisuje type! 'Life' + 'of' + description becomes categorized lifestyle. Specific kind of existence. 'A life of' pattern turns abstract into describable category. Wealth lifestyle characterization - 'a' introduces type of life lived.

items.question: _____ knowledge is power - the more you learn, the stronger you become mentally.
items.translation: (Wiedza jest potęgą - im więcej się uczysz, tym silniejszy stajesz się mentalnie)
items.explanation: Zero dla famous proverb! 'Knowledge is power' - established saying bez przedimka. Abstract concepts w axioms. Universal wisdom o learning's value. Proverbs maintain zero articles - formulaic expression unchanged.

items.question: She needs _____ courage to speak up in front of large audiences publicly.
items.translation: (Potrzebuje odwagi żeby przemówić przed dużymi audiencjami publicznie)
items.explanation: Zero dla 'needs courage' - requiring quality! Abstract virtue needed bez przedimka. Courage as necessary attribute nie countable thing. 'Need/require' + abstract quality standard pattern. Character requirement expressed - what's konieczne within.

items.question: _____ beauty is in the eye of the beholder - everyone has different preferences.
items.translation: (Piękno jest w oku patrzącego - każdy ma różne preferencje)
items.explanation: Zero dla philosophical proverb! 'Beauty is subjective' - famous saying. Abstract aesthetic concept discussed generally. Proverb maintains traditional form bez przedimka. Universal truth o subjective nature - concept-level wisdom statement.

items.question: He felt _____ sense of pride when his daughter graduated from university successfully.
items.translation: (Czuł poczucie dumy kiedy jego córka ukończyła uniwersytet pomyślnie)
items.explanation: 'A sense of pride' dla experienced emotion! 'Sense of' + abstract czyni instance of feeling. Specific moment's emotion. Abstract staje się experiential event kiedy 'sense of' used. Parent joy characterized - 'a' introduces particular emotional experience at moment.

name: Causative 'have/get' - zaawansowane
description: Have it done vs get it done - niuanse

items.question: I need to _____ my hair _____ before the wedding ceremony next weekend.
items.translation: (Muszę obciąć włosy przed ceremonią ślubną w przyszły weekend)
items.explanation: 'Have my hair cut' = someone cuts it FOR you! Causative: arrange dla someone else do action. Hairdresser cuts, nie you. To salon service - professional styling przed ważnym eventem. 'Have' + object + past participle dla usług professionals provide.

items.question: She finally _____ her old computer _____ by a professional technician yesterday.
items.translation: (W końcu naprawiła swój stary komputer przez profesjonalnego technika wczoraj)
items.explanation: 'Got it fixed' = caused repair to happen! 'Get' + object + past participle dla arranging actions. Technician fixed, ona arranged. To o delayed maintenance - finally getting necessary work done. 'Got' suggests effort w obtaining service - more active than passive 'had'.

items.question: We should _____ the house _____ before putting it on the market for sale.
items.translation: (Powinniśmy pomalować dom przed wystawieniem go na sprzedaż)
items.explanation: 'Have the house painted' = hire painters! Causative dla home improvement. Professionals do painting, wy arrange. To real estate prep - making property attractive. 'Have' + object + past participle standard dla hiring work done before sale.

items.question: I _____ my car _____ every 10,000 kilometers for maintenance and safety.
items.translation: (Serwisuję mój samochód co 10,000 kilometrów dla utrzymania i bezpieczeństwa)
items.explanation: 'Have it serviced' dla regular professional maintenance! Mechanic services, wy schedule. Routine care pattern - preventive maintenance. To responsible ownership - regular professional attention. 'Have' + serviced standard dla recurring professional vehicle care.

items.question: Can you _____ this package _____ to my office address by tomorrow morning?
items.translation: (Czy możesz wysłać tę paczkę na adres mojego biura do jutra rano?)
items.explanation: 'Get package delivered' = arrange delivery! 'Get' + object + past participle dla causing service. Courier delivers, wy arrange. To urgent shipping request - timing critical. 'Get' emphasizes obtaining result - active pursuit of delivery completion by deadline.

items.question: They _____ their house _____ into the property last night while sleeping!
items.translation: (Włamano im się do domu wczoraj w nocy podczas snu!)
items.explanation: 'Had house broken into' dla involuntary causative! Nie wanted ale happened TO them (victims). Negative causative - unwanted event experienced. To crime reporting - victimization. 'Had' shows passive reception nieprzyjemnego - suffered action bez desire.

items.question: I'm going to _____ my eyes _____ next week - I've been having vision problems.
items.translation: (Zbadam oczy w przyszłym tygodniu - mam problemy ze wzrokiem)
items.explanation: 'Have eyes tested' = get professional examination! Optometrist tests, wy schedule appointment. Medical checkup causative. To health maintenance - addressing symptoms. 'Have' + tested standard dla medical evaluations - arranging professional assessment.

items.question: She _____ her phone screen _____ after dropping it on concrete pavement.
items.translation: (Wymieniła ekran telefonu po upuszczeniu go na betonowy chodnik)
items.explanation: 'Got screen replaced' dla repair service! Technician replaces, ona pays. Accident damage fix. To smartphone repair - common mishap. 'Got' suggests effort w finding service - actively pursuing fix after damage occurred.

items.question: We need to _____ the contract _____ by a professional lawyer before signing it.
items.translation: (Musimy przejrzeć kontrakt przez profesjonalnego prawnika przed podpisaniem go)
items.explanation: 'Have contract reviewed' = obtain legal opinion! Lawyer reviews, wy commission. Due diligence - professional evaluation před commitment. To smart business - expert assessment before binding agreement. 'Have' + reviewed dla seeking professional analysis.

items.question: I finally _____ my tooth _____ out at the dentist after months of pain.
items.translation: (W końcu wyrwałem ząb u dentysty po miesiącach bólu)
items.explanation: 'Had tooth taken out' dla dental procedure! Dentist extracts, wy undergo. Medical necessity - pain resolution. To relief po prolonged suffering. 'Had' dla medical procedures performed ON you - receiving treatment rather than self-action.

items.question: They're _____ their wedding photos _____ professionally in a nice studio downtown.
items.translation: (Robią sobie zdjęcia ślubne profesjonalnie w ładnym studio w centrum)
items.explanation: 'Having photos taken' = photographer shoots! Professional service - posing while photographer works. Wedding preparation ritual. To o hiring expertise dla special occasion. 'Having' + taken standard dla photography services - subjects of photos, not photographers.

items.question: She _____ her watch _____ last week, but it's broken again already unfortunately!
items.translation: (Naprawiła swój zegarek w zeszłym tygodniu, ale znowu jest zepsuty już niestety!)
items.explanation: 'Had watch repaired' ale unsuccessful long-term! Watchmaker fixed ale problem recurred. Frustrating - paid dla repair że didn't last. To consumer complaint - poor service quality. Repair failed utrzymać się - wasted money na ineffective fix.

items.question: You should _____ your resume _____ by a career expert before applying widely.
items.translation: (Powinieneś przejrzeć swoje CV przez eksperta karierowego przed aplikowaniem szeroko)
items.explanation: 'Have resume reviewed' = get professional feedback! Career coach evaluates, wy receive input. Job search optimization. To smart preparation - expert eyes ulepszają document. 'Have' + reviewed dla seeking professional opinion before important submissions.

items.question: I _____ my blood pressure _____ regularly every few months at clinic.
items.translation: (Badam moje ciśnienie krwi regularnie co kilka miesięcy w klinice)
items.explanation: 'Have blood pressure checked' dla routine health monitoring! Nurse checks, wy maintain schedule. Preventive healthcare. To responsible health management - regular screening. 'Have' + checked standard dla medical monitoring - ongoing professional assessment habit.

items.question: They _____ their old furniture _____ when they moved to smaller apartment.
items.translation: (Wyrzucili swoje stare meble kiedy przeprowadzili się do mniejszego apartamentu)
items.explanation: 'Had furniture removed' = hired removal! Movers took away, oni arranged. Downsizing necessity - no room dla old pieces. To moving logistics - professional removal service. 'Had' + removed dla disposing przez hired help during relocation.

name: Wariacje 'such' w zdaniach skutkowych
description: Such a, such an, such... that patterns

items.question: It was _____ a wonderful surprise that I couldn't stop smiling all day long!
items.translation: (To była taka wspaniała niespodzianka że nie mogłem przestać uśmiechać się cały dzień!)
items.explanation: 'Such a' + adjective + singular countable! 'Such a wonderful surprise' - 'such' modifies całą noun phrase. Extreme positivity causing lasting joy. To o overwhelmingly good unexpected event. 'Such' z rzeczownikami, 'so' z przymiotnikami alone - kluczowa różnica.

items.question: She has _____ an amazing talent for languages that she learns them incredibly fast!
items.translation: (Ma taką niesamowitą zdolność do języków że uczy się ich niesamowicie szybko!)
items.explanation: 'Such an' przed vowel sound! 'An amazing talent' - 'such an' + adjective + noun. Exceptional ability producing rapid learning. To o linguistic gift - natural aptitude. 'Such an' dla singular nouns starting z vowels - phonetic rule within 'such' structures.

items.question: They're _____ kind people that everyone in neighborhood loves them dearly.
items.translation: (Są tak miłymi ludźmi że wszyscy w sąsiedztwie ich uwielbią serdecznie)
items.explanation: 'Such kind people' z plural! 'Such' + adjective + plural noun. No 'a/an' z plural. Exceptional kindness earning universal affection. To community beloved residents - character creating popularity. 'Such' modifies plural noun phrase showing collective quality.

items.question: It was _____ beautiful weather that we decided to eat lunch outside today.
items.translation: (Była taka piękna pogoda że zdecydowaliśmy zjeść lunch na zewnątrz dzisiaj)
items.explanation: 'Such beautiful weather' - uncountable noun! 'Such' + adjective + uncountable bez 'a'. Perfect conditions inspiring outdoor dining. To spontaneous plan change - weather so nice couldn't waste indoors. 'Such' z uncountables (weather/music/advice) bez przedimka.

items.question: He made _____ a silly mistake that he felt embarrassed in front of everyone.
items.translation: (Popełnił tak głupi błąd że poczuł się zawstydzony przed wszystkimi)
items.explanation: 'Such a silly mistake' causing embarrassment! Error type (silly) emphasized przez 'such a'. Foolish nature creating shame. To o avoidable errors - simplicity czyni worse. 'Such a' categorizes mistake type - highlighting szczególnie embarrassing nature.

items.question: There was _____ confusion about the schedule that nobody knew where to go!
items.translation: (Było takie zamieszanie o harmonogramie że nikt nie wiedział dokąd iść!)
items.explanation: 'Such confusion' - uncountable chaos! 'Such' + uncountable noun describing disorder level. Total organizational failure - nobody informed properly. To event mismanagement - lack of clear communication. 'Such' emphasizes degree of organizational chaos produced.

items.question: They showed _____ enthusiasm for the project that it inspired everyone else too!
items.translation: (Pokazali takie zapalenie dla projektu że zainspirowało to wszystkich innych też!)
items.explanation: 'Such enthusiasm' = uncountable high energy! 'Such' + uncountable emotion. Infectious excitement spreading. To o motivating team - their energy elevating others. 'Such' z emotion nouns creating inspirational ripple effect through group.

items.question: It's _____ a shame that you can't join us for dinner tonight at restaurant!
items.translation: (To taka szkoda że nie możesz dołączyć do nas na kolację dziś wieczorem w restauracji!)
items.explanation: 'Such a shame' = strong regret expression! Fixed emotional phrase. 'Such a' + emotion noun dla emphatic disappointment. To sincere wish oni could come - genuine sadness o absence. Idiomatic 'such a shame/pity' standard dla expressing regret.

items.question: She gave _____ good advice that I followed it exactly and succeeded!
items.translation: (Dała tak dobrą radę że zastosowałem się dokładnie i odniosłem sukces!)
items.explanation: 'Such good advice' - uncountable wisdom! 'Such' + adjective + uncountable. Counsel quality leading do positive outcome. To grateful acknowledgment - guidance worked. 'Such' z 'advice' (uncountable) emphasizing quality że produced success.

items.question: He has _____ expensive tastes that he can barely afford his lifestyle honestly.
items.translation: (Ma takie drogie gusta że ledwo stać go na jego styl życia szczerze)
items.explanation: 'Such expensive tastes' - plural preferences! 'Such' + adjective + plural. Champagne tastes na beer budget. To o financial strain - preferences exceeding means. Living beyond affordable - 'such' highlights mismatch między desires i resources.

items.question: There's _____ strong evidence against him that conviction seems almost certain now.
items.translation: (Jest tak mocny dowód przeciw niemu że skazanie wydaje się prawie pewne teraz)
items.explanation: 'Such strong evidence' - uncountable proof! 'Such' + adjective + uncountable. Overwhelming proof predicting verdict. To legal assessment - case strength. 'Such' z 'evidence' (uncountable) showing quantity i quality sufficient dla likely conviction.

items.question: It was _____ a relief to finally finish that difficult stressful project!
items.translation: (To była taka ulga w końcu skończyć ten trudny stresujący projekt!)
items.explanation: 'Such a relief' = huge emotional release! 'Such a' + feeling noun. Burden lifted creating strong positive emotion. To completion joy - stress ending brings profound relief. Idiomatic 'such a relief' standard dla expressing major emotional release po struggle.

items.question: He showed _____ disrespect to his teacher that he was sent to principal immediately.
items.translation: (Pokazał taki brak szacunku dla swojego nauczyciela że został wysłany do dyrektora natychmiast)
items.explanation: 'Such disrespect' - uncountable rudeness! 'Such' + uncountable negative behavior. Extreme impoliteness requiring punishment. To discipline scenario - behavior crossing line. 'Such' emphasizes severity co triggered immediate administrative response - beyond acceptable.

items.question: There were _____ interesting books in the library that I didn't know which to choose!
items.translation: (Było tak wiele interesujących książek w bibliotece że nie wiedziałem którą wybrać!)
items.explanation: 'Such interesting books' - plural abundance! 'Such' + adjective + plural. Overwhelming choice - too many good options. To paradox of choice - abundance creating indecision. 'Such' z plurals showing quantity i quality both excessive - delightful problem.

items.question: She received _____ praise for her performance that she felt truly appreciated finally.
items.translation: (Otrzymała takie pochwały za swój występ że poczuła się naprawdę doceniona w końcu)
items.explanation: 'Such praise' - uncountable acclaim! 'Such' + uncountable positive feedback. Abundant recognition creating validation. To artist fulfillment - work acknowledged generously. 'Such' z 'praise' (uncountable) showing volume i warmth of appreciation received.

name: Intensyfikatory i osłabiacze
description: Absolutely, fairly, rather, quite, pretty

items.question: I'm _____ sure I locked the door, but let me check once more to be certain.
items.translation: (Jestem całkiem pewien że zamknąłem drzwi, ale pozwól sprawdzić jeszcze raz żeby być pewnym)
items.explanation: 'Fairly sure' = reasonably confident, nie absolute! 'Fairly' osłabia - moderate certainty. Not 100% ale probable. To expressing doubt while leaning tak - qualified confidence. 'Fairly' tworzy space dla checking - confident enough ale prudent verification wise.

items.question: The exam was _____ difficult - even the best students struggled with several questions.
items.translation: (Egzamin był dość trudny - nawet najlepsi studenci mieli problem z kilkoma pytaniami)
items.explanation: 'Rather difficult' wzmacnia negatives! 'Rather' stronger than 'quite' - emphasizing difficulty był notable. Top students struggled proves challenge level. To validating students' concerns - acknowledging test was genuinely tough. 'Rather' adds sympathy - wasn't imagination, was actually hard.

items.question: This coffee is _____ hot - you should let it cool down before drinking it.
items.translation: (Ta kawa jest dość gorąca - powinieneś pozwolić jej ostygnąć przed piciem)
items.explanation: 'Pretty hot' = dość gorąco (informal)! 'Pretty' casual intensifier - moderately strong. Warning level - careful but nie extreme. To friendly caution - suggesting wait but nie alarming. 'Pretty' + adjective common w potocznym - softer than 'very'.

items.question: I'm _____ exhausted after running the full marathon race this morning early.
items.translation: (Jestem całkowicie wyczerpany po przebiegnięciu całego maratonu dziś rano wcześnie)
items.explanation: 'Absolutely exhausted' maksymalnie wzmacnia! 'Exhausted' już extreme więc 'absolutely' appropriate. Total depletion - marathon aftermath. To o complete physical drain. Extreme adjectives (exhausted/freezing/starving) demand absolute intensifiers - matching intensity levels.

items.question: The movie was _____ boring - I almost fell asleep several times during it.
items.translation: (Film był dość nudny - prawie zasnąłem kilka razy podczas niego)
items.explanation: 'Rather boring' negative wzmocnienie! 'Rather' podkreśla unpleasant quality - było notably dull. Almost sleeping proves serious tedium. To honest negative review - 'rather' softens criticism slightly ale makes clear it was genuinely boring experience.

items.question: She's _____ good at math, though not the absolute best in entire class.
items.translation: (Jest całkiem dobra w matematyce, chociaż nie najlepsza absolutnie w całej klasie)
items.explanation: 'Quite good' = wystarczająco dobrze! 'Quite' z gradables means 'moderately/reasonably'. Good ale nie outstanding. To balanced assessment - competent but recognizing others better. 'Quite' creates moderate praise - positive ale realistic, avoiding exaggeration.

items.question: The instructions are _____ clear - anyone can follow them without any problems.
items.translation: (Instrukcje są krystalicznie jasne - każdy może je śledzić bez żadnych problemów)
items.explanation: 'Perfectly clear' = maksymalna jasność! 'Perfectly' absolute intensifier dla completeness. Zero ambiguity - ultimate clarity achieved. To praising excellent documentation - accessibility dla all. 'Perfectly' emphasizes ideal state - couldn't be clearer.

items.question: I'm _____ certain he's lying about his qualifications and work experience claims.
items.translation: (Jestem całkowicie pewien że kłamie o swoich kwalifikacjach i twierdzeniach o doświadczeniu)
items.explanation: 'Absolutely certain' = zero doubt! Maximum confidence w assessment. Evidence overwhelmingly suggests dishonesty. To serious accusation - complete conviction o deception. 'Absolutely' eliminates wriggle room - not suspicion ale certainty based na facts.

items.question: The presentation was _____ interesting, better than I initially expected honestly.
items.translation: (Prezentacja była dość interesująca, lepsza niż początkowo oczekiwałem szczerze)
items.explanation: 'Quite interesting' = pleasantly moderate! 'Quite' pokazuje positive surprise - better than feared. Exceeded low expectations. To relieved assessment - wasn't dreading ale turned out engaging. 'Quite' qualified praise - good ale not amazing, satisfactory positive.

items.question: This puzzle is _____ impossible - I've been trying for hours without success!
items.translation: (Ta łamigłówka jest całkowicie niemożliwa - próbuję od godzin bez sukcesu!)
items.explanation: 'Absolutely impossible' maksymalna frustration! 'Impossible' extreme więc 'absolutely'. Hours failing proves extreme difficulty. To exasperated complaint - hyperbole o challenge level. May not literally impossible ale feels that way - 'absolutely' expresses defeated feeling.

items.question: She was _____ upset when she heard the disappointing news about rejection.
items.translation: (Była dość zdenerwowana kiedy usłyszała rozczarowujące wiadomości o odrzuceniu)
items.explanation: 'Rather upset' wzmacnia negative emotion! 'Rather' emphasizes distress was significant. Rejection news hurting. To empathetic observation - acknowledging legitimate emotional pain. 'Rather' validates feelings - wasn't overreaction, genuinely hurtful.

items.question: The hotel room was _____ clean and well-maintained throughout our entire stay.
items.translation: (Pokój hotelowy był idealnie czysty i dobrze utrzymany przez cały nasz pobyt)
items.explanation: 'Spotlessly clean' = perfekcyjna czystość! 'Spotlessly' specjalny intensifier dla 'clean'. Immaculate condition. To praising hotel standards - exceptional housekeeping. Special intensifiers exist dla specific adjectives - 'spotlessly clean', 'brand new', 'crystal clear'. Fixed collocations.

items.question: I'm _____ tired, but I can still help you if you really need assistance.
items.translation: (Jestem dość zmęczony, ale wciąż mogę ci pomóc jeśli naprawdę potrzebujesz pomocy)
items.explanation: 'Pretty tired' casual moderate wzmocnienie! 'Pretty' informal dla 'dość'. Tired ale nie exhausted - can still function. To qualified offer - acknowledging limitation ale maintaining availability. 'Pretty' creates honest ale helpful response - tired ale willing.

items.question: The explanation was _____ confusing - I didn't understand most of it at all.
items.translation: (Wyjaśnienie było dość mylące - nie zrozumiałem większości wcale)
items.explanation: 'Rather confusing' emphasizes negative quality! Clarity lacking significantly. 'Rather' strengthens complaint - wasn't slightly unclear ale notably confusing. To feedback o poor communication - most content incomprehensible. 'Rather' validates struggle - confusion justified.

items.question: She's _____ brilliant at solving complex mathematical problems quickly and accurately.
items.translation: (Jest absolutnie genialna w rozwiązywaniu złożonych problemów matematycznych szybko i dokładnie)
items.explanation: 'Absolutely brilliant' maksymalna pochwała! 'Brilliant' już strong więc 'absolutely'. Exceptional mathematical talent. To high compliment - emphasizing extraordinary ability. Speed + accuracy = brilliance confirmed. 'Absolutely' leaves no doubt - outstanding skill unquestionable.

name: Fronting zaawansowany dla emfazy
description: Never have I, Rarely does, Little did...

items.question: Seldom _____ we encounter such dedicated and hardworking students in our classes.
items.translation: (Rzadko spotykamy tak oddanych i pracowitych studentów w naszych klasach)
items.explanation: 'Seldom do we' odwraca dla emphasizing rarity! Negatywne 'seldom' na początku requires inwersję. Exceptional students uncommon. To teacher appreciation - highlighting unusual excellence. Inwersja dodaje weight do observation - these students stand out dramatically przez their rarity.

items.question: Not only _____ he speak perfect English, he also writes poetry beautifully!
items.translation: (Nie tylko mówi perfekcyjnie po angielsku, także pisze poezję pięknie!)
items.explanation: 'Not only does he' tworzy build-up! Inwersja po 'not only' prepares dla 'but also'. First skill impressive, second nawet more so. To o multitalented person - each revelation increases admiration. Structure builds excitement - fronting first achievement przed revealing additional.

items.question: Only when the results came _____ we realize how much we had actually improved!
items.translation: (Dopiero kiedy wyniki przyszły zdaliśmy sobie sprawę jak bardzo faktycznie się poprawiliśmy!)
items.explanation: Po 'Only when...' stosujemy inwersję (did + podmiot + czasownik). Przesunięcie przysłówka ograniczającego na początek zdania wymaga odwrócenia szyku zdania, co podkreśla zaskoczenie odkryciem.

items.question: Little _____ she know that this meeting would change her career path completely!
items.translation: (Mało wiedziała że to spotkanie zmieni jej ścieżkę karierową całkowicie!)
items.explanation: 'Little did she know' tworzy dramatic irony! Nie znała significance w moment ale narrator/audience zna. Storytelling device. To career pivot story - innocent meeting became turning point. Inwersja dodaje suspense - podkreśla how unaware była of life-changing nature.

items.question: Hardly _____ I finished speaking when everyone started asking difficult questions!
items.translation: (Ledwo skończyłem mówić kiedy wszyscy zaczęli zadawać trudne pytania!)
items.explanation: 'Hardly had I finished when' pokazuje immediate sequence! Past perfect + inwersja po 'hardly'. Barely completed przed bombardment. To o overwhelming response - no breathing room. Timing emphasis - instant transition od presenting do questioning.

items.question: Never in my life _____ I seen such breathtaking natural beauty anywhere before!
items.translation: (Nigdy w życiu nie widziałem takiego zapierającego dech naturalnego piękna gdziekolwiek wcześniej!)
items.explanation: 'Never in my life have I' maksymalna emfaza! 'Never' + time phrase + inwersja. Lifetime unprecedented experience. To expressing awe - nothing comparable w entire existence. Multiple emphasis elements: never + life + inversion = extraordinary statement.

items.question: Under no circumstances _____ you reveal this confidential sensitive information!
items.translation: (W żadnych okolicznościach nie powinieneś ujawniać tej poufnej wrażliwej informacji!)
items.explanation: 'Under no circumstances should' categorical prohibition! Negatywna phrase + inwersja tworzy authoritative command. Absolute secrecy requirement. To security warning - zero tolerance. Inwersja czyni rule sound non-negotiable - emphatic structure reinforces seriousness.

items.question: Not until midnight _____ they finally finish all the urgent work for deadline.
items.translation: (Dopiero o północy w końcu skończyli całą pilną pracę na termin)
items.explanation: 'Not until midnight did they' podkreśla late completion! Inwersja po 'not until' emphasizes timing. Pracowali do very late hour. To o dedication lub poor planning - working do extreme hours. Late hour emphasized przez inversion - unusual timing noteworthy.

items.question: Only by working together as a team _____ we achieve our ambitious goals successfully.
items.translation: (Tylko pracując razem jako zespół osiągniemy nasze ambitne cele pomyślnie)
items.explanation: 'Only by teamwork can we' podkreśla necessity! 'Only by' + method + inwersja shows to JEDYNY way. Cooperation essential - individuals insufficient. To rallying call - emphasizing collaboration criticality. Inwersja czyni message persuasive - structure reflects importance.

items.question: Rarely _____ such generous offers come along - you should accept it immediately!
items.translation: (Rzadko takie hojne oferty się pojawiają - powinieneś zaakceptować natychmiast!)
items.explanation: 'Rarely do offers come' emphasizes opportunity rarity! Inwersja po 'rarely' podkreśla unusual nature. Exceptional chance - don't hesitate. To urgent advice - scarcity creates value. Rarity emphasized przez fronting + inversion - compelling acceptance.

items.question: Not a single person _____ complain about the new flexible working policy introduced.
items.translation: (Ani jedna osoba nie narzekała na nową elastyczną politykę pracy wprowadzoną)
items.explanation: 'Not a single person did complain' podkreśla unanimous approval! Complete absence skarg. Zero complaints = universal satisfaction. To policy success indicator - nobody unhappy. Absolute descriptor + inwersja emphasizes totality - highlighting complete absence of dissent.

items.question: So tired _____ she that she fell asleep immediately during the boring lecture.
items.translation: (Tak zmęczona była że zasnęła natychmiast podczas nudnego wykładu)
items.explanation: 'So tired was she' fronts adjective dramatically! 'So' + adjective + inwersja tworzy literary effect. Extreme exhaustion level. To explaining sleeping - wasn't rudeness ale physical necessity. Dramatic structure emphasizes intensity że made sleep unavoidable.

items.question: At no point during interview _____ she mention her impressive past achievements.
items.translation: (W żadnym momencie podczas wywiadu nie wspomniała o swoich imponujących przeszłych osiągnięciach)
items.explanation: 'At no point did she mention' podkreśla complete omission! Negatywna time phrase + inwersja. Never brought up - surprising modesty. To o humble candidate - didn't self-promote despite credentials. Omission noteworthy - fronting emphasizes unusual restraint.

items.question: No sooner _____ we arrived at beach than it started raining heavily unfortunately!
items.translation: (Ledwo przybyliśmy na plażę kiedy zaczęło mocno padać niestety!)
items.explanation: 'No sooner had we arrived than' perfect bad timing! Fixed structure: no sooner + past perfect + than. Immediate unfortunate coincidence. To Murphy's law - worst timing. Barely arrived przed rain ruined plans. Structure podkreśla frustrating proximity events.

items.question: Only in this way _____ you fully understand the complex problem we're facing now.
items.translation: (Tylko w ten sposób w pełni zrozumiesz złożony problem z którym się borykamy teraz)
items.explanation: 'Only in this way can you' podkreśla method exclusivity! 'Only' phrase + inwersja shows this IS the path. No alternatives work. To pedagogical insistence - specific approach necessary dla comprehension. Inwersja makes methodology sound essential - emphasizing uniqueness of effective approach.

name: Conditional Perfect Continuous
description: Would have been doing - niezrealizowane ciągłe akcje

items.question: If I hadn't stopped him, he _____ still _____ about his problems all day!
items.translation: (Gdybym go nie powstrzymał, wciąż by narzekał o swoich problemach cały dzień!)
items.explanation: 'Would be complaining' pokazuje ciągłą action która była by in progress! Hypothetical ongoing state. Stopped (reality) prevented continued complaining (avoided scenario). To o interrupting annoying behavior - without intervention, would STILL be going. Continuous emphasizes duration że była by endless.

items.question: Without the GPS, we _____ still _____ around lost right now in circles!
items.translation: (Bez GPS wciąż byśmy jeździli zagubieni właśnie teraz w kółko!)
items.explanation: 'Would be driving' hypothetical present continuous! GPS saved (reality) od continued wandering (avoided scenario). Still lost NOW if no GPS. To grateful dla technology - prevented prolonged confusion. Present hypothetical shows co was by happening at this very moment.

items.question: If she had accepted the job offer, she _____ _____ there for five years now already!
items.translation: (Gdyby przyjęła ofertę pracy, pracowałaby tam już od pięciu lat teraz!)
items.explanation: 'Would have been working' mixed hypothetical! Past decision (didn't accept) affecting hypothetical present duration. If accepted THEN, would BE working NOW for 5 years accumulated. To career path reflection - alternative timeline visualization. Perfect continuous dla accumulated hypothetical tenure.

items.question: Without your constant interruptions, I _____ _____ by now and relaxing!
items.translation: (Bez twoich ciągłych przerywań, już bym skończył i odpoczywał!)
items.explanation: 'Would have finished by now' pokazue completed hypothetical! Interruptions (reality) delayed completion (consequence). Would BE done already if uninterrupted. To complaint o productivity loss - distractions prevented earlier finish. 'By now' emphasizes temporal loss - should be done but isn't.

items.question: If it weren't raining, we _____ _____ outside in the garden right now enjoying sun!
items.translation: (Gdyby nie padało, siedzielibyśmy na zewnątrz w ogrodzie właśnie teraz ciesząc się słońcem!)
items.explanation: 'Would be sitting' hypothetical present continuous! Rain prevents (reality) od outdoor enjoyment (desired scenario). Would BE outside NOW if sunny. To weather regret - imagining better conditions. Present continuous hypothetical paints vivid picture of alternative present moment.

items.question: He _____ still _____ if we hadn't explained the rules clearly to him repeatedly.
items.translation: (Wciąż by się mylił gdybyśmy nie wyjaśnili zasad wyraźnie jemu wielokrotnie)
items.explanation: 'Would be making mistakes' ongoing hypothetical errors! Explanation (reality) prevented continued errors (avoided scenario). Would STILL be wrong without teaching. To o intervention effectiveness - clarification stopped ongoing problem. Continuous shows errors was by persistent bez help.

items.question: If I had started learning earlier, I _____ _____ English for ten years by now!
items.translation: (Gdybym zaczął uczyć się wcześniej, uczyłbym się angielskiego już przez dziesięć lat do teraz!)
items.explanation: 'Would have been learning' hypothetical accumulated duration! Earlier start (didn't happen) would mean longer study period accumulated BY NOW. To regret o delayed beginning - calculating lost time. Perfect continuous dla hypothetical accumulated experience - what duration WOULD BE.

items.question: Without the alarm clock, I _____ still _____ right now instead of working!
items.translation: (Bez budzika wciąż bym spał właśnie teraz zamiast pracować!)
items.explanation: 'Would be sleeping' hypothetical present state! Alarm woke (reality) prevented continued sleep (avoided scenario). Would STILL be unconscious NOW. To grateful dla wake-up call - bez niego missed obligations. Present continuous hypothetical shows ongoing state that would persist.

items.question: She _____ _____ much longer if the doctor hadn't given her proper treatment quickly.
items.translation: (Cierpiałaby znacznie dłużej gdyby lekarz nie dał jej odpowiedniego leczenia szybko)
items.explanation: 'Would have been suffering' hypothetical prolonged pain! Treatment (reality) ended suffering earlier than would have naturally. To medical intervention appreciation - doctor shortened ordeal. Perfect continuous dla extended hypothetical discomfort - imagining że was by if untreated.

items.question: If they had invested wisely earlier, they _____ _____ profits for years now already!
items.translation: (Gdyby zainwestowali mądrze wcześniej, zbieraliby zyski już od lat teraz!)
items.explanation: 'Would have been collecting' hypothetical ongoing gains! Wise investment (didn't happen) would mean accumulated years of returns BY NOW. To financial regret - calculating missed compound earnings. Perfect continuous dla accumulated hypothetical benefits over extended period.

items.question: We _____ still _____ for a solution if you hadn't come up with this brilliant idea!
items.translation: (Wciąż byśmy szukali rozwiązania gdybyś nie wpadł na ten genialny pomysł!)
items.explanation: 'Would be searching' ongoing hypothetical struggle! Your idea (reality) ended search (avoided scenario). Would STILL be stuck NOW without insight. To crediting breakthrough - appreciating że ended fruitless hunt. Present continuous hypothetical emphasizes persistent problem że was resolved.

items.question: If the meeting hadn't been cancelled, we _____ _____ there for three hours now!
items.translation: (Gdyby spotkanie nie zostało odwołane, siedzielibyśmy tam przez trzy godziny teraz!)
items.explanation: 'Would have been sitting' hypothetical endurance! Cancellation (reality) uratowała od prolonged meeting (avoided scenario). Would be tam for 3 hours BY NOW if proceeded. To relief o dodging tedious obligation. Perfect continuous shows accumulated sitting time że was by endured.

items.question: He _____ still _____ if his friends hadn't convinced him to stop finally.
items.translation: (Wciąż by palił gdyby jego przyjaciele nie przekonali go żeby w końcu przestał)
items.explanation: 'Would be smoking' hypothetical ongoing habit! Friends' intervention (reality) ended smoking (avoided continuation). Would STILL smoke NOW without peer pressure. To appreciating intervention - friends' concern changed behavior. Present continuous hypothetical shows bad habit że was by persisting.

items.question: If she had known the truth earlier, she _____ _____ differently all this time!
items.translation: (Gdyby znała prawdę wcześniej, zachowywałaby się inaczej cały ten czas!)
items.explanation: 'Would have been acting differently' hypothetical altered behavior! Earlier knowledge (didn't have) would changed whole behavior pattern. To regret o delayed truth - ignorance caused inappropriate actions. Perfect continuous dla extended hypothetical different conduct across time period.

items.question: Without modern medicine, many people _____ still _____ from diseases we can cure now.
items.translation: (Bez nowoczesnej medycyny, wiele ludzi wciąż by cierpiało na choroby które możemy leczyć teraz)
items.explanation: 'Would be suffering' hypothetical ongoing affliction! Medical advances (reality) eliminated suffering (prevented scenario). Would STILL be sick NOW without cures. To appreciating medical progress - gratitude dla eliminated diseases. Present continuous hypothetical shows persistent illness że medicine ended.

name: Wariacje zdań rozłupanych
description: All I want is, The thing that, It's... that

items.question: All _____ I need right now is a hot shower and comfortable bed to sleep in.
items.translation: (Wszystko czego potrzebuję teraz to gorący prysznic i wygodne łóżko żeby spać)
items.explanation: 'All that I need' minimalizuje request! 'All' + 'that' emphasizes simplicity - only THESE things. Modest wants - basic comfort. To expressing tiredness - minimal needs statement. 'All that' structure focuses na essential bare requirements - nothing fancy.

items.question: The reason _____ I'm calling is to discuss our upcoming important meeting agenda.
items.translation: (Powodem dla którego dzwonię jest omówienie naszej nadchodzącej ważnej agendy spotkania)
items.explanation: 'The reason why' introduces purpose clearly! Fronting 'reason' followed by explanation. Business call opening - stating intent upfront. To professional communication - clarifying call purpose immediately. 'Why' connects reason noun do its specific explanation clause.

items.question: _____ really matters is your effort and attitude, not just natural talent alone.
items.translation: (Co naprawdę ma znaczenie to twój wysiłek i postawa, nie tylko naturalny talent sam)
items.explanation: 'What really matters' prioritizes values! Fronts importance assessment. Effort over talent - motivational message. To encouraging trying - downplaying innate ability importance. 'What matters' structure identifies true priorities - separating essential od superficial.

items.question: It wasn't the price _____ bothered me, it was the terrible customer service received.
items.translation: (To nie cena mnie niepokoiła, to okropna obsługa klienta otrzymana)
items.explanation: 'It wasn't... that' corrective cleft! Clarifying true complaint - not cost ale treatment. To consumer complaint focus shift - price acceptable, behavior not. Contrasting structure separates assumed problem od real issue - correcting misunderstanding o grievance.

items.question: The thing _____ I love most about traveling is meeting new interesting people everywhere.
items.translation: (Rzecz którą najbardziej kocham w podróżowaniu to spotykanie nowych ciekawych ludzi wszędzie)
items.explanation: 'The thing that I love' casual highlighting! Fronts preferred aspect. Human connections over sights. To o travel philosophy - people make experiences memorable. 'The thing that' conversational alternative do formal 'what' - friendly sharing of preferences.

items.question: It's your dedication, not luck, _____ has brought you this far in career successfully.
items.translation: (To twoje oddanie, nie szczęście, które zaprowadziło cię tak daleko w karierze pomyślnie)
items.explanation: 'It's dedication... that' credits true source! Contrasting structure - dedication vs luck. Attributing success correctly - effort not chance. To motivational - affirming merited achievement. 'That' connects emphasized factor (dedication) do its result - proper credit assignment.

items.question: _____ I don't understand is why he made that strange decision so suddenly.
items.translation: (Czego nie rozumiem to dlaczego podjął tę dziwną decyzję tak nagle)
items.explanation: 'What I don't understand' fronts confusion! Emphasizes mystery przed revealing specific puzzle. To seeking explanation - declaring bafflement before stating question. 'What' packages incomprehension jako subject - building anticipation dla what exactly puzzles.

items.question: It was because of your help _____ I managed to pass the difficult exam successfully!
items.translation: (To dzięki twojej pomocy udało mi się zdać trudny egzamin pomyślnie!)
items.explanation: 'It was because of help that' attributes success! Isolating causative factor - your assistance. Grateful acknowledgment - crediting support. To thanking properly - specific attribution of success do helper's role. Cleft emphasizes causal connection clearly.

items.question: All _____ matters to me is that everyone is safe and healthy right now.
items.translation: (Wszystko co ma dla mnie znaczenie to że wszyscy są bezpieczni i zdrowi właśnie teraz)
items.explanation: 'All that matters' minimizes concerns! Only ONE thing important - safety/health. Crisis perspective - priorities clarified. To reassessment - material things irrelevant compared do wellbeing. 'All that' focuses exclusively na what truly counts.

items.question: The place _____ I feel most relaxed and comfortable is my own cozy home.
items.translation: (Miejsce gdzie czuję się najbardziej zrelaksowany i komfortowo to mój własny przytulny dom)
items.explanation: 'The place where I feel' identifies sanctuary! Fronting location emphasis. Home as ultimate comfort zone. To o belonging - nowhere else matches. 'Where' connects place noun do feeling description - geographical-emotional link establishing home's unique value.

items.question: It was in 1969 _____ humans first landed on the moon surface successfully.
items.translation: (To było w 1969 kiedy ludzie po raz pierwszy wylądowali na powierzchni księżyca pomyślnie)
items.explanation: 'It was in 1969 that' emphasizes date! Historical fact highlighting - year fronted. Momentous achievement timing. To educational - teaching significant date. Cleft structure makes year prominent - time emphasis dla milestone event.

items.question: What I _____ is some peace and quiet to think clearly about this decision.
items.translation: (Czego potrzebuję to trochę spokoju i ciszy żeby myśleć jasno o tej decyzji)
items.explanation: 'What I need is' fronts necessity! Emphasizes requirement przed revealing what. Simple request - conducive environment. To asking dla space - mental clarity requires calm. 'What I need' structure prioritizes need itself przed specifying object.

items.question: The way _____ he treated her was completely unacceptable and very rude honestly.
items.translation: (Sposób w jaki ją potraktował był całkowicie nieakceptowalny i bardzo niegrzeczny szczerze)
items.explanation: 'The way that he treated' describes manner! Fronting method emphasis. Behavior evaluation - condemning approach. To criticism - manner was wrong. 'The way that' focuses na HOW something was done - method assessment struktura.

items.question: It's not what you know _____ matters most, it's who you know in business.
items.translation: (To nie co wiesz ma największe znaczenie, to kogo znasz w biznesie)
items.explanation: 'It's not X that matters, it's Y' contrasting structure! Knowledge vs connections - cynical business truth. To o networking importance - relationships trump expertise. Cleft comparison highlighting controversial reality - contacts valued over competence.

items.question: _____ we should focus on now is solving this immediate urgent problem first.
